type: micro-SaaS / B2B tool (AI App Store screenshot generator)
asking_price: $5,000 (TrustMRR; cut from $9,000 within ~4 days of listing; '0.8x' = last-30d revenue annualized — vs 1.03x on MRR)
one_line_reason: A 10-week-old AI screenshot generator with real early traction (30 subs, $405 MRR) being flipped at the top of its launch curve — episodic-need product with subscription pricing (MRR growth +7% vs revenue +51% = churn already showing), zero moat in a saturated category, distribution = the founder's X audience, and a $5k ask that is 1.67x budget after a 44% price cut in days.

operability_upside.note: Exact stack fit (Next.js/TypeScript/Supabase/Stripe/OpenAI). Real roadmap: Play Store support, localization, App Store Connect upload (AppScreens' differentiator), template library. But engineering upside is capped by a commodity category.

pmf_retention.note: 30 paying subs in ~10 weeks is genuinely decent conversion velocity. Structural problem: screenshot need is episodic (launch/major update), so subscriptions churn once the job is done. Evidence it's already happening: revenue +51.2%/30d but MRR only +7.1%; ARPU $13.50 is below the cheapest $19 tier (discounts or annual mixing).

unit_economics.note: 85% margin claimed, OpenAI per-generation costs modest. But episodic value dressed in subscription pricing → likely 2-3 month effective lifetimes; LTV maybe $40-90 against unknown CAC. One-time-purchase competitors (AppMockUp Studio) anchor pricing.

moat_defensibility.note: Saturated: AppScreens, AppMockUp, AppLaunchpad, Previewed, LaunchMatic, Screenshots Pro, Hotpot, free Figma templates — plus general AI image tools commoditizing the core. DR 2.0, no brand, no data. Minor plus: appears in a few third-party 2026 roundup listicles.

growth_market.note: App-store tooling is mature/flat; AI simultaneously enables the product and destroys its reason to exist as a standalone (any dev can prompt a general design tool). Single-purpose AI wrappers are under structural squeeze.

tech_ip.note: Simple modern stack, OpenAI dependency swappable, should transfer cleanly. Two founders (Salim + Irere) — both must convey IP. Unverified; site itself is behind aggressive bot protection (403), un-auditable from outside.

transfer_key_person.note: Distribution rides the founder's X audience (3,181 followers, build-in-public) — TrustMRR even lists the follower count as a selling point. Listicle placements transfer; the audience does not.

deal_terms.note: $5,000 ≈ 1.03x annualized MRR — fair-to-cheap on paper, but 1.67x the $3,000 ceiling with no financing. Seller cut from $9,000 to $5,000 within ~4 days and reports 3 offers — motivated, possibly negotiable to budget, but the competing offers cut the other way.

seller_adverse_selection.note: Selling a 10-week-old product at the top of its launch curve, with a 44% price cut days after listing, stalling MRR vs headline revenue, ARPU below sticker, and no stated reason for sale. The seller has seen the churn cohorts we can't.

key_unknowns: Cohort retention — the deal-decider: of the 30 subs, how many from the first month are still active? Episodic-need churn would confirm the flip thesis; genuine multi-month retention would be the one genuinely bullish surprise.
Why ARPU ($13.50) is below the cheapest tier ($19) — annual discounting, promo codes, or grandfathered pricing; changes true MRR quality.
Actual channel mix — if meaningful signups come from listicles/organic search rather than the founder's X, growth_lever_fits could flip; DR 2 makes it unlikely.
Why the price was cut $9,000→$5,000 within ~4 days of listing, and why sell at 10 weeks old at all — no reason stated.
Whether both founders (Salim and cofounder Irere) are on the sale and IP assignment.
What the 3 existing offers are — the budget gate stands unless the price lands ≤$3,000-3,750.
seller_questions: Share monthly cohort retention since launch: of April/May subscribers, how many are still active today? Export the Stripe subscription list (read-only) so we can reconcile. — resolves: pmf_retention / revenue_evidence → buyer_verified; why: screenshots are an episodic need — MRR +7% vs revenue +51% suggests churn is already offsetting new signups.

Why is average revenue per subscription ($13.50) below your cheapest $19 tier — annual plans, discounts, or legacy pricing? Break down the 30 subs by plan and billing period. — resolves: unit_economics; why: the effective price sets true LTV.

Where do signups come from — X, Product Hunt, the comparison listicles, search? Share the last 60 days of traffic/signup sources. — resolves: growth_lever_fits / value_transfers; why: if demand is your 3,181-follower X audience, it leaves with you.

Why did the asking price drop from $9,000 to $5,000 within days of listing, and why sell a 10-week-old growing product at ~1x ARR? — resolves: seller_adverse_selection; why: the insider's urgency is information.

Are both you and your cofounder party to the sale, and will both sign the IP assignment? Any third-party code, templates, or assets without clear license? — resolves: ip_transferable; why: two-founder micro-deals fail on the missing signature.

What are monthly running costs (OpenAI, Supabase, Vercel/hosting, domain), and what does one generation cost in OpenAI credits at current volume? — resolves: unit_economics; why: verifies the 85% margin claim.

Would you consider ~$3,000, or an earnout tied to MRR retention at 3-6 months? — resolves: deal_terms / affordability; why: asking is 1.67x the buyer's ceiling and the retention risk is the whole question — an earnout prices it.
inputs_provided: Listing URL trustmrr.com/startup/appshots (user) — VERIFIED as existing

TrustMRR .md profile figures: $2,111 all-time, $555 last-30d (+51.2%), $405 MRR (+7.1%), 30 active subs, 85% margin, DR 2.0, $5,000 asking, 0.8x stated, 200 recent viewers, 3 offers, listed 2026-07-13, founded 2026-04-30, US, tiers $19/$29/$49, founder Salim (3,181 X followers) + cofounder Irere, Stripe-verified badge — MARKETPLACE_ATTESTED
Prior shortlist entry (leads/trustmrr-shortlist.md, corrected 2026-07-14): asking $9,000, 30d revenue $517 — VERIFIED (own record); implies ~44% price cut within days
ARPU inconsistency: 30 subs × $19 min tier = $570 > $405 reported MRR — VERIFIED arithmetic flag
Competitive set: AppScreens, AppMockUp, AppLaunchpad, Previewed, LaunchMatic, Screenshots Pro + free tools — VERIFIED (public sources); AppShots appears in some 2026 roundups
appshots.me returned HTTP 403 to automated fetch (bot protection) — product not independently inspected
Budget ceiling $3,000 / no financing — reused from session convention

scorecard_markdown:
# Acquisition Scorecard (v2) — AppShots (appshots.me)

**Type:** micro-SaaS / B2B tool (AI App Store screenshot generator)  ·  **Asking:** $5,000 (cut from $9,000 within ~4 days; '0.8x' = 30d revenue annualized, 1.03x on MRR)  ·  **Budget:** $3,000  ·  **Date:** 2026-07-18
**Figures reconciled?** two-source ✓ (listing .md + own prior shortlist) · verify_figures ✓ arithmetic — but **ARPU flag:** 30 subs × $19 min tier = $570 > $405 reported MRR · revenue evidence: marketplace_attested
**Sources reviewed:** TrustMRR .md profile, prior corrected shortlist (Jul 14), competitor roundups (AppScreens, AppMockUp, AppLaunchpad, Previewed, LaunchMatic); appshots.me itself blocked automated inspection (403)

## Verdict

> **PASS (provisional) — 42/100, OUT OF BUDGET** (completeness 66%)
> A 10-week-old product being flipped at the top of its launch curve. The traction is real by micro-standards (30 subs, $405 MRR, $2.1k all-time since April 30) — but the shape is wrong on every axis that matters: **growth-lever gate (verified) caps it at PASS** (demand rides the founder's 3,181-follower X audience and roundup listicles; DR 2 rules out owned SEO); the need is **episodic** — devs subscribe to make launch screenshots, then cancel — and the listing already shows it (revenue +51%/30d but MRR only +7.1%, ARPU $13.50 below the cheapest $19 tier); the category is **saturated** (AppScreens, AppMockUp one-time-purchase, AppLaunchpad, Previewed, LaunchMatic, free Figma templates, general AI image tools); and the **$5,000 ask is 1.67x the $3,000 ceiling** even after a 44% price cut days into listing — which, together with selling at 10 weeks old, is the adverse-selection tell. Biggest swing factor: cohort retention — real multi-month retention would surprise upward, but even then the channel and budget gates hold.

## Scorecard (nine dimensions)

| # | Dimension | Wt | Score /5 | Conf | Weighted | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Operability & product upside | 15 | 3 | med | 9.0 | Exact stack; real roadmap (Play Store, localization, Store Connect upload). Upside capped by commodity category. |
| 2 | PMF & retention | 15 | 2 | med | 6.0 | 30 subs in 10 weeks is decent velocity; episodic need + stalling MRR growth say the bucket leaks. |
| 3 | Unit economics & upside | 12 | 2 | med | 4.8 | 85% margin claimed; episodic value in subscription clothing → short lifetimes; one-time competitors anchor price. |
| 4 | Moat & defensibility | 12 | 1 | high | 2.4 | Saturated field + AI commoditization; DR 2; minor listicle presence. |
| 5 | Growth & market | 8 | 2 | med | 3.2 | Mature tooling niche; AI is an existential squeeze on single-purpose wrappers. |
| 6 | Tech & IP health | 8 | 3 | low | 4.8 | Simple transferable stack (assumed); two founders must both sign; site un-auditable (403). |
| 7 | Transfer / key-person | 10 | 2 | med | 4.0 | The X audience is the channel and it leaves with Salim; listicles transfer. |
| 8 | Deal terms & value | 10 | 2 | high | 4.0 | ~1x MRR-ARR is fair-to-cheap, but 1.67x budget, no financing; $9k→$5k cut + 3 offers = motivated seller. |
| 9 | Seller & adverse-selection | 10 | 2 | med | 4.0 | Flip at peak launch curve, 44% cut in days, no stated reason, stalling MRR — the seller has seen the cohorts. |
| | **Merit total** | **100** | | | **42.2** | |

**Gates:** operable ✓ · growth-lever-fits ✗ (caps → PASS) · revenue-evidence marketplace_attested (provisional) · value-transfers ? · IP-transferable ? · not-fragile ? · **affordable** NO ($5,000 vs $3,000 → OUT OF BUDGET)

## Fit-to-build read (the team's lens)

The build side is trivially in the wheelhouse — a Next.js/Supabase/OpenAI app the team could write from scratch in weeks, with an obvious engineering roadmap (Store Connect upload, localization, Play Store). That is precisely the problem: the code is worth little, the category is crowded with better-funded and free alternatives, and 100% of the value creation left is audience-building and content marketing for indie developers — the founder's X-and-listicles game, not this team's.

## Does the value transfer? (key-person check)

TrustMRR lists the founder's X follower count as a headline stat — the audience *is* the asset, and it doesn't convey. What transfers: code, domain (DR 2), 30 subscriptions of unknown remaining lifetime, and a few listicle backlinks. The honest transfer question is retention: how much of the $405 MRR survives 90 days under an owner who isn't posting about it?

## What we know vs. what we're assuming

- **Verified (buyer-reconciled):** the price cut ($9,000 on Jul 14 shortlist → $5,000 by Jul 18); the ARPU/tier mismatch ($13.50 vs $19 minimum); the competitive saturation; MRR-vs-revenue growth divergence.
- **Assumed / marketplace-attested:** all dollar figures (Stripe badge); 85% margin; 30 subs; product quality (site blocked inspection).
- **Unknown (and how it swings):** cohort retention (the deal-decider — good retention ≈ +8-10 merit pts, still PASS-capped by channel); channel mix (could flip growth_lever_fits, unlikely at DR 2); cofounder sign-off; the 3 competing offers; price flexibility to ≤$3,750.

## Seller questions to send (prioritized)

1. Cohort retention since launch: of April/May subscribers, how many are active today? Read-only Stripe export to reconcile. — *resolves: pmf / revenue → buyer_verified; MRR +7% vs revenue +51% suggests churn offsetting signups.*
2. Why is ARPU ($13.50) below your cheapest $19 tier? Break down the 30 subs by plan and billing period. — *resolves: unit_economics.*
3. Signup sources for the last 60 days — X, PH, listicles, search? — *resolves: growth_lever / value_transfers.*
4. Why the $9,000→$5,000 cut within days, and why sell at 10 weeks old? — *resolves: adverse-selection.*
5. Are both founders signing the sale and IP assignment? Any unlicensed third-party assets? — *resolves: ip_transferable.*
6. Monthly running costs and OpenAI cost per generation? — *resolves: unit_economics (85% margin check).*
7. Would you take ~$3,000, or an MRR-retention earnout at 3-6 months? — *resolves: affordability + prices the retention risk.*

## Recommended next step

**Pass.** The one number that could surprise (cohort retention) doesn't clear the two gates that are already verified: the growth engine is a personal X audience the team can't and won't replicate, and the price is 1.67x budget with three competing offers pushing it the wrong way. The $9k→$5k cut in four days says the seller already knows what the retention curve looks like. Same corpus lesson as ShowFindr and AgentIdeaHub — at this price band you're buying the founder's launch spike, and the spike isn't transferable.
